pros: Greenfield build opportunity reporting to CRO. Consumption-based pricing aligns with the candidate's desired metric framework. Remote-first with modern tech stack (Salesforce, Gainsight, Slack, Mac). Series D with $425M raised suggests stability with growth runway. Team of 8-12 is an ideal size.
cons: Series D may mean less equity upside than earlier stage. Enterprise sales transition could mean heavy process buildout before greenfield innovation. No mention of AI-native strategy. Need to verify founder type and leadership culture.
greenfield_rationale: Explicitly described as building the GTM operating system from scratch during a PLG-to-enterprise transition.
recommended_angle: Lead with the AcmeSaaS NRR story — scaling GTM ops during a pricing model transition is directly transferable.
_scoring_breakdown: base 3.5 + Layer-4 positives (greenfield, modern_tech, modern_pricing, target_sector, remote) stacked past the +2.5 cap = 6.0 deterministic. LLM adjustment 0.0. A strong-fit role, but under ruthless scoring the deterministic ceiling is 6.0 — an 8+ requires the Layer-2 corpus match plus the top-band gate's must-haves.

pros: RevOps leadership scope; reports to CFO; renewal-process focus.
cons: Windows/Teams shop; hybrid 3 days in office; process-cleanup (upcycling) role, not greenfield.
greenfield_rationale: Optimizing existing Salesforce/renewal processes — maintenance, not net-new build.
sector: Healthcare Technology (HealthTech SaaS — allowed; not Traditional Healthcare)
_scoring_breakdown: HealthTech SaaS is allowed (the example profile blocks only Traditional Healthcare: hospital systems, pharma, managed care). No auto-reject. base 3.5 + windows_penalty(-2.0) = 1.5 deterministic.

job_title: Head of GTM Strategy and Operations
pros: Newly created greenfield role reporting to COO. DevTools sector with strong growth (80% YoY). Pricing transition from seat to consumption — the candidate can architect the operational model. Financial rigor emphasis in the JD directly matches a CPA background. Fully remote, modern stack, meaningful equity.
cons: 200 employees is at the lower end of the target range. Pricing transition is complex and could consume all bandwidth for 12-18 months. Need to verify founder type and CS team health.
greenfield_rationale: Newly created role with a mandate to build the GTM operating model from scratch during a pricing model transition.
recommended_angle: Lead with the financial-rigor angle — a CPA who can build board-ready reporting while designing consumption-era GTM systems.
_scoring_breakdown: base 3.5 + Layer-4 positives (greenfield, modern_tech, target_sector, remote, modern_pricing) stacked past the +2.5 cap = 6.0 deterministic. LLM adjustment 0.0. Whether this clears into the 7+ band depends on the Layer-2 corpus match and the top-band gate.

pros: Good tech stack (Salesforce, Slack, Mac). Sacramento-based hybrid is convenient geography. Series B with 150 employees is within target company size. Analytics sector is adjacent to AI/Data.
cons: Reports to VP of Sales, not C-level — limited strategic authority. Scope is narrow: pipeline reporting, territory management, quota setting. No mention of CS or full GTM mandate. Salary range ($185k-$210k) is above floor but below target. No equity mentioned. No greenfield signals.
greenfield_rationale: Series B may have greenfield needs, but the JD scope is narrowly operational (pipeline/territory/quota). Limited blue-ocean opportunity visible.
recommended_angle: If pursuing, frame as an opportunity to expand scope beyond Sales ops into full GTM operations — but verify appetite for that during the recruiter screen.
_scoring_breakdown: base 3.5 + modern_tech(+1.0) = 4.5 deterministic. No greenfield, no consumption pricing, no sector bonus (generic analytics). LLM -0.2 for narrow scope and a VP-Sales reporting line — final 4.3.
Auto-rejected: Gambling sector is in the hard_no list (ethics). Would have scored well on comp and remote otherwise, but the ethics filter fires first.
